summary: The plan and dispatch contract are structurally sound, accurately reflect the codebase at HEAD, and the FP class is reliably reproducible via the documented fixtures. Both architectural reframes (A: duplicate types span two files; B: TaintSinkType is insufficient and needs +4 additive variants) are verified against source. However, four discrete issues require either an explicit pre-launch fix or a tightening of the dispatch contract: (1) test_taint_sink_type_variants in taint_tests.rs:202 hard-codes variants.len()==6 and is NOT mentioned in plan or contract — it will deterministically RED on M1 cargo test --workspace; (2) plan §1 marks vuln.rs::TaintSource/TaintSink for deletion but plan §5.2 reads VulnFinding.source.variable/expression — these are the deleted struct's fields; the plan must explicitly state whether these output-record structs are retained-with-fields-from-canonical-projection or actually deleted with VulnFinding's source/sink field types becoming canonical taint::TaintSource/TaintSink (which would be a CLI breaking change at remaining/vuln.rs:679-688); (3) R1 'public API extension breaks external consumers' is materially overstated — there are ZERO exhaustive matches on TaintSinkType anywhere in crates/ at HEAD (verified: only matches!() and ::filter() comparisons exist; bugbot's text_format.rs match is on &str not the enum); compile-time blast radius is the test in (1) and the existing variant-list-update tests; (4) plan code sketch §3-M3 references build_codemap() which does not exist — extract_functions(tree, source, language) at tldr-core/src/ast/extractor.rs:120 returns Vec<String> only (no line ranges); building the per-function (start_line, end_line) range required by plan §5.2 needs extract_functions_detailed which is private — the executor will need to either expose it or hoist the fn-range derivation from cfg.blocks (per the precedent at tldr-cli/src/commands/taint.rs:92-103). Beyond these, performance baseline is plausible (single-file Go HEAD wall-clock ~9ms; <2x = ~18ms is tight but feasible; large files with many small functions are the at-risk class). M5 atomic boundary is correctly scoped. Recommend launching /autonomous after the dispatch contract is amended with an additional validator_mandate covering these four items; do not block on full plan rewrite.

tigers.name: test_taint_sink_type_variants_will_fail_in_M1
tigers.evidence: crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint_tests.rs:202 — `assert_eq!(variants.len(), 6);`. M1 adds 4 variants; cargo test --workspace will fail this assertion deterministically. Also test_taint_source_type_variants at L180 asserts variants.len()==6 but TaintSourceType is NOT being extended — that one stays GREEN.
tigers.blast_radius: M1 stop_threshold says 'cargo check --workspace passes' and 'cargo clippy passes' but NOT 'cargo test --workspace passes'. The variant-count test isn't a compile error — it's a runtime assertion at test time. M1 enumerates RED tests but this test is in the GREEN-already harness, not the RED harness.
tigers.mitigation: M1 green_files MUST add an update to taint_tests.rs:202: change `assert_eq!(variants.len(), 6)` to `assert_eq!(variants.len(), 10)` and extend the variants[] array with HtmlOutput/FileOpen/HttpRequest/Deserialize. Add this to dispatch contract M1.green_files.

tigers.name: plan_contradiction_TaintSource_TaintSink_deletion_vs_VulnFinding_field_reads
tigers.evidence: plan §1 line 84-85 says 'DELETE TaintSource (vuln.rs:68)' and 'DELETE TaintSink (vuln.rs:81)'. plan §5.2 lines 474, 476, 477, 478, 480, 481 read VulnFinding.source.variable, VulnFinding.source.expression, VulnFinding.sink.function, VulnFinding.sink.expression — i.e., the FIELDS of the structs marked for deletion. tldr-cli/src/commands/remaining/vuln.rs:679-688 also reads f.source.expression, f.source.source_type, f.sink.expression, f.sink.sink_type — same deleted-struct fields.
tigers.blast_radius: Either (a) the structs are NOT actually deleted but renamed / kept as output-record wrappers populated via Into<canonical::TaintSource>, in which case plan §1 wording is wrong; OR (b) the structs ARE deleted and VulnFinding's source/sink field types become canonical::TaintSource/TaintSink — this changes JSON output shape (canonical uses `var: String`/`statement: Option<String>` vs vuln.rs's `variable: String`/`expression: String`), which violates validator_mandate cli_output_shape_unchanged AND requires updating the CLI conversion site at remaining/vuln.rs:679-688.
tigers.mitigation: Dispatch contract M3.green_files must explicitly state which arm of the contradiction applies. Recommend (a): retain vuln::TaintSource/TaintSink as 'output-record adapter structs' with their existing field shapes (variable/source_type/expression/line for source; function/sink_type/expression/line for sink), populated via From<canonical::TaintSource> impls. This preserves both JSON shape AND the CLI conversion site. The plan §1 'DELETE' wording should be relabeled 'COLLAPSE INTO CANONICAL VIA From-projection' and §5.2's table is the correct intent.

tigers.name: build_codemap_does_not_exist
tigers.evidence: plan §3-M3 sketch line 233 says `let codemap = build_codemap(&source, language)?;` and `for fn_def in codemap.functions { ... fn_def.name ... within_fn_range(i, &fn_def) ... }`. There is no build_codemap fn or Codemap type in tldr-core. extract_functions(tree, source, language) at tldr-core/src/ast/extractor.rs:120 returns Vec<String> (names only, no ranges). extract_functions_detailed at ast/extract.rs:206 returns Vec<FunctionInfo> with ranges but is `fn` (private).
tigers.blast_radius: Executor will hit a missing-symbol error mid-M3 and either spin on figuring out the right API or invent its own. The proven precedent at tldr-cli/src/commands/taint.rs:92-103 derives (fn_start, fn_end) from cfg.blocks AFTER calling get_cfg_context(file, fn_name, lang) — but that requires already knowing the fn_name, which is what we're trying to enumerate.
tigers.mitigation: M3.green_files must specify the function-discovery API. Two options: (i) make extract_functions_detailed pub (one-line change), call it from scan_file_vulns, iterate FunctionInfo entries; (ii) call existing pub extract_functions to get names, then call get_cfg_context per-name and derive ranges from cfg.blocks (more calls but no API surface change). Recommend (i) — already-tested helper, exposes for the proper consumer.

elephants.name: R1_public_api_extension_risk_is_overstated
elephants.evidence: Verified by `grep -rn 'match.*sink_type\|match.*TaintSinkType' crates/ --include='*.rs'`: ZERO exhaustive matches on TaintSinkType. text_format.rs:276 is `match sink_type` where sink_type is `&str` (line 268: `.as_str()`), not the enum. All other usages are `matches!(s.sink_type, TaintSinkType::ShellExec)` (filter pattern, not exhaustive) or constructor sites (`sink_type: TaintSinkType::SqlQuery`). Adding 4 variants is API-additive AND match-additive — no compile errors anywhere except the variant-count test (T1).
elephants.implication: The plan's §8 R1 'HIGH if not careful, LOW with mitigation' framing is too pessimistic. Reality: even WITHOUT the enumeration audit, M1 cargo check passes; only T1 surfaces a problem. This actually relaxes the M1 burden — no per-site explicit-arm extension needed. Note for executor: skip the predicted '~3 sites in tldr-cli/commands/taint.rs + bugbot/l2/ir.rs + tests' enumeration in dispatch contract; those sites are fine.

elephants.name: perf_baseline_2x_threshold_is_tight_for_many_small_function_files
elephants.evidence: tldr vuln /tmp/vuln-mig-repro/string_literal_fp.go --lang go --format json wall-clock at HEAD: ~9ms (3-line `time` measurement, single file, 2 functions). Per-function CFG+DFG+SSA construction is amortized over fn body but invoked PER function. A file with 30 small functions × ~3ms cfg/dfg/ssa overhead each = 90ms — 10x baseline. The plan's <2x threshold gates on the 20-file Go fixture average, not the worst-case file — so 'average' could pass while specific files regress 10x.
elephants.implication: Performance assertion needs sharpening: contract should require BOTH (a) average <2x across the 20-file fixture, AND (b) p99 file <5x baseline. Otherwise the threshold passes on average but the user's actual experience on a hot-path file craters.

elephants.name: perf_fixture_directory_does_not_exist_yet
elephants.evidence: validator_mandate performance_baseline_captured_m1 says: 'M1 captures wall-clock baseline on a canonical 20-file Go fixture set (path: continuum/autonomous/vuln-migration-v1-plan/perf-fixture/)' — directory does not exist at HEAD. M1 must create it as part of red_tests work.
elephants.implication: M1 deliverables list does not call out 'create perf-fixture/ with 20 representative Go files'. Without it, perf baseline is non-reproducible and M3 stop-threshold gate is meaningless. Add to M1.additional_artifacts.

elephants.name: external_publish_post_milestone_binary_verification_not_planned
elephants.evidence: is_final_internal_milestone: true; closes_issues_at_release lists 5 GitHub issues. M5 binary verification covers the 3 FP-repro fixtures + 132 fixture corpus. But after vuln-migration-v1 lands, before `cargo publish`, what additional verification gates the publish? Not specified in plan §10 or §11.
elephants.implication: The publish IS the action that closes the listed issues. If publish carries an unverified regression, all 5 issues 'close' on a broken release. Add post-tag pre-publish gate: (i) tldr vuln on a recent real-world repo (e.g., a tldr-code itself OR a known-tainted Python project) producing expected output; (ii) tldr taint regression suite (which the canonical pipeline drives) GREEN; (iii) full `cargo doc --no-deps -p tldr-core` clean (no broken doc-link warnings on new TaintSinkType variants).

elephants.name: ast_sink_bank_218_count_is_a_top_estimate_not_a_target
elephants.evidence: plan §M2 line 126 estimates 'Xss ~9 langs × 3 patterns = 27; PathTraversal 16 langs × 5 patterns = 80; Ssrf ~9 langs × 8 patterns = 72; Deserialization 13 langs × 3 patterns = 39. Total ~218 new AstSinkPattern entries.' Cross-checked against vuln.rs L381-L778 per-language tables — the source of truth for the patterns. Some languages have empty tables for some VulnTypes (per coverage matrix in plan §4.2), so the actual count may be lower. The dispatch contract M2 should say 'AT LEAST one AstSinkPattern entry per (lang, sink_type) where vuln.rs's table is non-empty' — match the audit verdict, not a fixed count.
elephants.implication: M2.stop_thresholds shouldn't gate on '218 entries' — gate on parity with vuln.rs's existing per-language tables (which is what plan §M2 says) plus M2-parity-audit.json verdict. Numerical claim is decorative.

elephants.name: compute_taint_per_function_is_more_than_thin_wrapper
elephants.evidence: tldr-cli/commands/remaining/vuln.rs:730-1500 (analyze_python_file region) is ~770 LOC of intra-procedural tree-walk. Per investigation, this is mostly redundant with canonical compute_taint_with_tree (which already does AST-based source/sink/sanitizer detection on the parsed tree). However, `is_string_interpolation_tainted` at L1570 does Python-specific f-string decomposition that may not have a 1:1 canonical equivalent. R7 (LOW-MEDIUM in plan) acknowledges this. Verified parity in canonical via grep for extract_interpolated_vars in taint.rs.
elephants.implication: If M4's parity check finds extract_interpolated_vars handles f-strings correctly, deletion is clean. If gap surfaces, the carry-forward is to extend canonical (NOT retain CLI-local). Plan §M4 line 320 already states this; sufficient. No change needed.

non_blocking_observations: vuln_rs_TaintSource_TaintSink_are_pub_struct_not_re_exported: They are reachable via the qualified path tldr_core::security::vuln::TaintSource only (mod.rs line 26 re-exports VulnType/VulnFinding/VulnReport, not the struct types). External consumers reading VulnFinding.source.variable do depend on these structs — preserve.

tldr_vuln_exit_code_at_runtime: tldr 0.3.0 (binary at /Users/cosimo/.local/bin/tldr) was confirmed to use RemainingError::findings_detected (exit 2) — code path verified in remaining/vuln.rs:516. Direct `time` invocation showed exit 0 due to shell-redirect interaction; the contract claim is correct.
Python_FP_clean_verified: `tldr vuln /tmp/vuln-mig-repro/string_literal_fp.py` returns 0 findings at HEAD. Plan's negative-control assertion holds.

Go_FP_count_verified: 3 false-positive CommandInjection findings, matching the plan's claim. Confirmed via `tldr vuln /tmp/vuln-mig-repro/string_literal_fp.go --lang go --format json | jq '.findings | length'`.
TS_FP_verified: 1 FP CommandInjection on fp2.ts; the cited 'sink' line at L5 is a comment (// (3) ...) — substring scanner does not strip comments. Plan claim verified.

16_AST_SINKS_banks_present: PYTHON_AST_SINKS through OCAML_AST_SINKS exist (verified via grep). Wiring at taint.rs:2914-2989 confirms 16 LanguagePatterns sub-banks. AST infrastructure for adding new sink-types is in place.

59_existing_AstSinkPattern_entries_at_HEAD: count via grep -c '^\s*AstSinkPattern {' crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs = 59. Plan's +218 new entries would bring the total to 277. Reasonable scale.

extract_functions_pub_helper_exists: tldr-core/src/ast/extractor.rs:120 — returns Vec<String> for 16 languages (CSharp returns empty since no free fns). Combined with get_cfg_context per-function call, this provides the function-discovery surface for M3 without new pub API. extract_functions_detailed at ast/extract.rs:206 returns Vec<FunctionInfo> with line ranges but is private — see T3.
no_TaintSinkType_exhaustive_match_anywhere: bugbot/text_format.rs:276 is the only `match sink_type` site and it's against &str. R1 'public API breaks consumers' is overstated — see E1.
M5_atomic_boundary_is_correctly_scoped: Atomic deletion of M3 helper-tests + M4 tracker-tests aligns with sanitizer-removal-v1 M4 ATOMIC pattern. Sequencing depends:[M4] enforced.

test_count_audit_36_obsolete_close: Verified vuln.rs has ~28 tests slated for deletion + remaining/vuln.rs has 4 = 32, slightly under the '~36' claim but within the contract's '~26' (M5 stop_thresholds line 304). Contract numbers are internally inconsistent (plan says 36, M5 stop_thresholds says 26) — should align.

specific_validations_performed.claim_taint_sink_type_6_variants: VERIFIED — taint.rs:153 has exactly 6 variants: SqlQuery (L155), CodeEval (L157), CodeExec (L159), CodeCompile (L161), ShellExec (L163), FileWrite (L165). Confirmed via `sed -n '150,170p'`.

claim_4_new_variants_no_overlap.HtmlOutput_for_Xss: NO overlap with existing 6. SqlQuery is for SQL execution; CodeEval/Exec/Compile are for code-as-data; ShellExec is for OS shell; FileWrite is for filesystem. None match HTML rendering. Variant name is precise.
claim_4_new_variants_no_overlap.FileOpen_for_PathTraversal: PARTIAL semantic overlap with FileWrite (both touch filesystem), but FileWrite is write-only ('open(..., "w")', '.write_text()'); FileOpen covers read+write+open() default mode. Plan §1 acknowledges 'FileWrite is too narrow (write-only; misses read+open)' — verified accurate. Distinct variant justified. Note: the projection in plan §3-M3 maps BOTH FileOpen AND FileWrite to PathTraversal vuln_type — so semantically these stay together at the user-facing level. Acceptable.
claim_4_new_variants_no_overlap.HttpRequest_for_Ssrf: NO overlap. None of the 6 existing variants cover outbound HTTP. Variant name is precise.
claim_4_new_variants_no_overlap.Deserialize_for_Deserialization: NO overlap. CodeEval and CodeExec cover code-as-string evaluation; Deserialize covers structured-data deserialization (pickle/yaml/Marshal/ObjectInputStream/unserialize) which is RCE-via-deserialization not RCE-via-eval. Variant name is precise.

specific_validations_performed.claim_218_ast_entries: PLAUSIBLE — vuln.rs L381-L778 contains the 16-language sink tables across 4 categories. Sample-counted: Python's get_sinks(Xss) returns ~6 entries, Python's get_sinks(PathTraversal) returns ~5 entries; rough extrapolation puts the additive total around 200-250. Plan's '218' estimate is within range. Recommend M2.stop_thresholds gate on parity-audit verdict, not fixed count.
specific_validations_performed.claim_taint_tracker_700_loc: VERIFIED — analyze_python_file at L730 + 9 helpers + is_taint_source/is_taint_sink/is_parameterized_query/is_string_interpolation_tainted/find_taint_in_string + vuln_type_name + get_python_parser. Region L730-L1500 = 770 LOC (close to '~700'). TaintTracker struct itself is L335-L390 (~55 LOC). Dispatch contract M4.green_files anchors are accurate.
specific_validations_performed.claim_python_fp_clean: VERIFIED via repro — tldr vuln /tmp/vuln-mig-repro/string_literal_fp.py returns {"findings":[]} (0 findings). Python tree-sitter analyze_python_file path is FP-clean for the substring class.
specific_validations_performed.claim_go_3_fps_at_head: VERIFIED via repro — tldr vuln /tmp/vuln-mig-repro/string_literal_fp.go --lang go --format json | jq '.findings | length' returns 3. Each finding cites a string-literal source line.
specific_validations_performed.exhaustive_match_sites_check: ZERO exhaustive matches on TaintSinkType in crates/. Verified: text_format.rs:276 matches on &str (sink_type from JSON), not the enum. All filter/matches!() sites compare to specific variants — adding more variants does not break those. R1 risk is low.
specific_validations_performed.obsolete_test_enumeration_complete: vuln.rs core: 5 bank-content tests (L1322-L2077) + 4 helper tests (L1227-L1305 propagation/extraction) + 7 type-coercion tests (L1366-L1412) + 12 sanitization tests (L1425-L1567) = ~28 tests. remaining/vuln.rs CLI: 4 tests at L1844/1862/1900/test_string_interpolation_detection. Total: ~32 obsolete tests. Plan/contract internally inconsistent (plan §1: ~36; M5 stop_thresholds: ~26; investigation: ~26). Recommend reconciliation in M1.M1-test-enumeration.json artifact (which is already gating per validator_mandate).
specific_validations_performed.performance_baseline: Single Go file at HEAD: ~9ms wall-clock (real time, `time tldr vuln ... --format json > /dev/null`). 2x threshold = ~18ms. Per-function CFG+DFG+SSA via compute_taint_with_tree precedent at taint.rs:128 has not been measured per-function in isolation. Estimate: a 30-function file could see 50-100ms (5-10x baseline). Recommend p99-file gate (E2).

risk_register.name: M1_test_taint_sink_type_variants_break
risk_register.trigger: M1 cargo test --workspace runs after the 4-variant addition
risk_register.mitigation: Add update of taint_tests.rs:202 to dispatch contract M1.green_files; also any other variant-enumerating tests (none found, but worth a grep before commit).

risk_register.name: M3_TaintSource_TaintSink_struct_disposition_ambiguity
risk_register.trigger: Executor mid-M3 must decide whether to delete vuln.rs structs or retain as output adapters
risk_register.mitigation: Amend plan/contract to explicitly state retention with From-projection; document as: 'tldr_core::security::vuln::TaintSource and ::TaintSink remain (as output-record adapters with their existing field shapes) — the canonical taint::TaintSource and taint::TaintSink replace them ONLY at the propagation boundary, not at the public API boundary. From<canonical::TaintSource> for vuln::TaintSource and From<canonical::TaintSink> for vuln::TaintSink are added in M3.'

risk_register.name: M3_function_discovery_API_missing
risk_register.delta_from_plan_R: NEW — plan code sketch references nonexistent build_codemap()
risk_register.trigger: Executor mid-M3 attempts to enumerate functions in a file
risk_register.mitigation: Choose: (i) make ast/extract.rs::extract_functions_detailed pub; or (ii) use ast/extractor.rs::extract_functions (Vec<String>) → loop get_cfg_context per name → derive (start, end) from cfg.blocks (precedent at tldr-cli/src/commands/taint.rs:92-103). Document which in M3.green_files.additions.

risk_register.name: M3_perf_p99_file_regression_not_gated
risk_register.trigger: M3 substring-to-canonical migration on a many-small-functions file
risk_register.mitigation: Strengthen M3 stop_threshold to: (avg <2x baseline) AND (p99-file <5x baseline). 20-file Go fixture must include a representative many-function file.

risk_register.name: post_publish_unverified_regression_propagates_to_users
risk_register.trigger: External cargo publish after vuln-migration-v1 tag, prior to additional regression suite
risk_register.mitigation: Add a 'pre-publish gate' artifact: post-M6 (after tag), but BEFORE cargo publish, run (a) tldr vuln on the tldr-code repo itself; (b) full taint test suite; (c) cargo doc --no-deps -p tldr-core. Capture to reports/pre-publish-binary-verification.json.

wave_safety_assessment.M1_red_tests_and_baseline: MOSTLY-SAFE with two amendments: (a) MUST update taint_tests.rs:202 test_taint_sink_type_variants to assert variants.len()==10 with the 4 new variants enumerated [DR1]; (b) MUST create perf-fixture/ directory with 20 representative Go files [E3]. Without (a), M1 cargo test fails. Without (b), M3 perf gate is meaningless.
wave_safety_assessment.M2_taint_sink_type_extension_safety: SAFE — variants additive, no exhaustive matches in codebase, ~218 estimate is plausible. Stop-threshold should gate on parity-audit verdict not fixed count.
wave_safety_assessment.M3_dispatch_routing_safety: MEDIUM-RISK — three structural issues to resolve before launch: (a) struct disposition contradiction [DR2]; (b) function-discovery API choice [DR3]; (c) p99-file perf gate [DR4]. None are blockers, all have clear mitigations.
wave_safety_assessment.M4_match_classification_safety: SAFE — analyze_python_file deletion straightforward; canonical extract_interpolated_vars covers f-string flow per regex-removal-v1 precedent; carry-forward shape is well-rehearsed. Plan §M4 line 320 already addresses parity guard.
wave_safety_assessment.M5_atomic_deletion_safety: SAFE — atomic boundary correctly scoped (mirrors sanitizer-removal-v1 M4); test count ~32 (per audit) within the contract's range; binary verification protocol is concrete (3 FP fixtures + 132-corpus + Python negative control). Reconcile plan §1 vs M5 stop_thresholds test count.
wave_safety_assessment.M6_changelog_tag_safety: SAFE — doc-only; CHANGELOG entry well-drafted in plan §6; tag local-only; no push/publish constraints honored.

external_publish_gate_assessment: After vuln-migration-v1 tag lands, prior to `cargo publish` that closes #7/#23/#24/#27/#28: recommended gate is a 4-step binary verification (NOT in current plan): (1) tldr vuln on tldr-code's own crates/ — capture findings count and shape; (2) tldr taint full regression suite via cargo test --workspace --features semantic; (3) cargo doc --no-deps -p tldr-core (verify new TaintSinkType variants documented); (4) tldr vuln smoke against a known-tainted real-world Python project (e.g., a Flask-based web app with intentional sources/sinks). Capture to reports/pre-publish-binary-verification.json. M6 should produce this artifact alongside the CHANGELOG, given is_final_internal_milestone=true. The plan does not currently prescribe this — it stops at the local tag.
discriminative_tests_recommended: Verify before /autonomous launch: rg 'variants\.len\(\) == 6' crates/ — should match only test_taint_sink_type_variants. Confirms T1's blast-radius.
Verify before /autonomous launch: rg 'match.*\.sink_type\b' crates/ — should match nothing structural; confirms E1's relaxation of R1.
Verify before /autonomous launch: rg 'extract_functions_detailed\b' crates/ — confirms it's pub or private (exact disposition affects DR3).
After M2 lands: rg 'AstSinkPattern \{' crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs | wc -l — expect 59 (HEAD) to grow by 200-250. If <100 added, parity audit incomplete.
After M3 lands: tldr vuln /tmp/vuln-mig-repro/string_literal_fp.go --lang go — must return 0 findings. 'Closes-#24-shaped' confirmed locally.
After M3 lands: time tldr vuln continuum/autonomous/vuln-migration-v1-plan/perf-fixture/ --lang go > /dev/null. Must be < 2x M1-baseline AND no individual file > 5x baseline.
After M5 lands: cargo test --workspace --features semantic 2>&1 | grep -c 'FAILED' — expect 0. If any non-zero, atomic boundary leaked.

conditions: AMEND dispatch-contract.json validator_mandates with new entry: 'taint_sink_type_variants_test_update_required' — M1.green_files must include update of crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint_tests.rs:202 to enumerate all 10 variants. (Addresses T1/DR1.)

AMEND plan §1 OR dispatch-contract.json M3.green_files: explicitly state vuln::TaintSource and vuln::TaintSink survive (as output-record adapters with existing field shapes — variable/source_type/expression/line and function/sink_type/expression/line); From<canonical::*> impls populate them. Plan §1 'DELETE' wording is misleading. (Addresses T2/DR2.)

AMEND dispatch-contract.json M3.green_files: specify function-discovery API choice: either (i) make ast/extract.rs::extract_functions_detailed pub, or (ii) use ast/extractor.rs::extract_functions + per-name get_cfg_context with cfg.blocks-derived ranges. (Addresses T3/DR3.)

AMEND dispatch-contract.json M1.additional_artifacts: explicitly add 'continuum/autonomous/vuln-migration-v1-plan/perf-fixture/ — 20 representative Go files spanning small-function and large-function shapes (target: 5 files with >10 functions each, 5 with 3-10 functions each, 10 with 1-3 functions each)'. (Addresses E3.)
AMEND dispatch-contract.json M3.stop_thresholds: replace single '<2x baseline' with '(avg <2x baseline) AND (p99-file <5x baseline)'. (Addresses DR4/E2.)
OPTIONAL: add dispatch-contract.json post-M6 'pre-publish-binary-verification' phase with 4 checks (E4/DR5). Could also be tracked as a separate handoff to the publish operator.

RECONCILE plan §1 (~36 obsolete tests), §M5 stop_thresholds (~26), and investigation.json (~26). M1.M1-test-enumeration.json should be the authority — recommend updating other locations to point at M1's enumeration.
dispatch_readiness.estimated_runtime_for_autonomous: M1: 60-90 min (132 fixtures + RED tests + variant extension + perf-fixture + taint_tests.rs:202 update). M2: 90-120 min (218 AST entries × small-edits, parity audit). M3: 120-180 min (largest single milestone; rewrite scan_file_vulns + projection helpers). M4: 90-120 min (delete ~770 LOC + analyze_file simplification). M5: 30-60 min (atomic test deletion + binary verification). M6: 15-30 min (CHANGELOG + tag). Total: 6-10 hours wall-clock for autonomous execution.
dispatch_readiness.rollback_complexity: moderate — M5 is atomic (single revert restores). M3 is the largest individual revert surface (~500 LOC + 8 helpers + scan_file_vulns rewrite). M2 is mechanical (218 additions, individually revertable). M1 is fully additive (revert deletes new files only). The validator_mandate retain-vuln_rs-as-module ensures the file isn't deleted, easing rollback.
